h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: it still feels like the odd card because it looks like something to work out
scored.statedInsightEvidence: 12.01 still pulls like a calculation, but it stays in the copy column.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue resisting or qualifying 12.01's label status as each new positional device was applied.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: sets 6, C, Carbon, and 12.01 under COPY FROM TILE; leaves CALCULATION empty
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the new sorting task introduced by the form device, correctly placing all four items including 12.01 under COPY FROM TILE and leaving CALCULATION empty, enacting a classification criterion that was not available in earlier turns.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The sleeve settled position. It did not settle the job of 12.01. Sort the four cards on this form. If the tile gives the mark directly, put it under COPY FROM TILE. If the mark has to be worked out, put it under CALCULATION. Then read the copy column for release.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 explicitly names the failure of the sleeve route ('The sleeve settled position. It did not settle the job of 12.01') and introduces a wholly different device — a two-column source-classification form — that shifts the learner's task from positional reading to source-type sorting, satisfying both the stock-taking contrast and the new public mechanism requirements.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Sort the four cards on this form. If the tile gives the mark directly, put it under COPY FROM TILE. If the mark has to be worked out, put it under CALCULATION. Then read the copy column for release.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The form device is precisely fitted to the learner's persistent blockage — the felt pull of 12.01 as a calculation — by giving the learner an actionable binary criterion (source type: copy vs. calculate) that is visibly different from the positional sleeve route and forces the learner to classify rather than merely read position.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The form settled the source. It did not settle the pull. Release gate now: the next bench reads only this tag, not the form. Write one handoff line for the bottom 12.01 that names it as the tile label, with no calculation verbs or work cues. If it sounds like a procedure, cross it out and write the label line.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 acknowledges the learner's L4 residual pull ('It did not settle the pull') and responds with a new output-register task — writing a handoff tag line free of calculation verbs — that directly addresses the learner's revised but still-qualified framing rather than simply continuing the form exercise.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Earth beside it still makes this feel less clean; Earth is a source too, just not for this upward arrow. Is that the right split?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue applying the blank-filling check to each remaining arrow in sequence, following the tutor's established object-source routine.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Force on cart from table, upward: this arrow. Force on cart from Earth, upward: no, that names the downward arrow, so Earth goes under OTHER FORCE. For the right arrow, the same check gives force on cart from hand, rightward.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically sorts the cards using the full-sentence gate criterion introduced by the tutor, correctly applying it to both the upward and rightward arrows without prompting.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The blank settled the push; it does not settle Earth and table together, because both can be sources but not for this same arrow. Use this gate: 'force on cart from ___, upward.' Move each source card into THIS ARROW only if the full sentence names this upward arrow; otherwise put it in OTHER FORCE.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 explicitly marks that the prior blank-filling device has stopped working for the two-source case (stock-taking contrast) and introduces a new sorting gate with a two-column physical representation that requires the learner to do different classificatory work (new public mechanism).
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Use this gate: 'force on cart from ___, upward.' Move each source card into THIS ARROW only if the full sentence names this upward arrow; otherwise put it in OTHER FORCE.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The gate is precise and directly fitted to the learner's live blockage — the ambiguity of two valid sources for different arrows — giving an actionable sentence-completion criterion and a physical sorting task that the learner can transfer to the remaining arrows, though it is slightly over-directed by pre-labelling the columns.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L3, so no recognition-contingent uptake is available to score.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The bar line still goes after the third tile, yes?
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The bar line stays after the third quarter tile. The tempo card stays in the other column. If it slides back toward the boxes, that is the error to catch.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on track to confirm the lower-box numeral and dismiss the tempo card, continuing the same confirmatory pattern established in L1–L3.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: sorts the 3 card, the 4 key, and the bar-line marker under BAR STRUCTURE; sets the tempo card sideways under PERFORMANCE DIRECTION
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 physically enacts the two-column classification device introduced by T3, completing the sort and articulating the error-detection criterion ('If it slides back toward the boxes, that is the error to catch'), which is a new representational action beyond mere acceptance.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The key has fixed the bottom number, but the face-up tempo card is still asking for a box. Before this sheet moves down the row, sort four pieces: top number, bottom number, one bar line, tempo card. After the sort, only the pieces under BAR STRUCTURE may touch the two boxes or the bar line.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 explicitly names the old route's failure ('the face-up tempo card is still asking for a box') and introduces a new public mechanism—the two-column check slip with a sorting task—that makes the learner do categorically different work (physical classification) rather than continuing the confirmatory numeral-checking route.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: places a two-column check slip above the worksheet: BAR STRUCTURE / PERFORMANCE DIRECTION, then turns the tempo card sideways beside the second heading … sort four pieces: top number, bottom number, one bar line, tempo card. After the sort, only the pieces under BAR STRUCTURE may touch the two boxes or the bar line.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is precisely fitted to the learner's specific blockage (tempo card's gravitational pull toward the boxes), gives an actionable exclusion criterion ('only the pieces under BAR STRUCTURE may touch'), and requires the learner to perform the classification rather than receive a correction, though it is slightly over-directed in naming all four pieces explicitly.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. The sheet now says 3/4 as bar structure; tempo stays visible, but outside the boxes.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 confirms the sorted state and ratifies the learner's error-detection framing but does not visibly change task, question, or evidence standard in response to L4's specific new contribution ('If it slides back toward the boxes, that is the error to catch'), making it a closing confirmation rather than a contingent adaptation.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: L4 physically enacts the two-column classification device introduced by T3, completing the sort and articulating the error-detection criterion ('If it slides back toward the boxes, that is the error to catch'), which is a new representational action beyond mere acceptance.
